catalysts_full.content:
# AMD — Catalysts

## TL;DR

Q1 2026 earnings on May 5 (after market close, 5 PM ET) is the live binary catalyst. Consensus revenue estimate: ~$9.84 billion (~32% YoY growth). Intel's Q1 2026 blowout (+22% Data Center & AI revenue, stock +24.89%) is a direct positive read-through for AMD's x86 AI CPU business. Four analyst upgrades arrived this week (DA Davidson Buy/$375, Susquehanna Buy/$375, Envision Research upgraded to Buy) versus one downgrade (Northland to Market Perform/$260). Stock at $353-357 now trades above the average consensus PT of ~$293, meaning significant optimism is already priced in. Beat-and-lower risk on Q2 guidance remains the primary earnings trap.

## Upcoming events

| Date | Event | Expected impact |
|---|---|---|
| May 5, 2026 (after close, 5 PM ET) | Q1 2026 earnings | Binary — revenue consensus ~$9.84B; EPS consensus ~$1.24-$1.27 |
| July 23, 2026 | AMD "Advancing AI 2026" flagship event, Moscone Center, San Francisco | Positive — product roadmap reveal; multi-session AI strategy event |
| 2H 2026 | MI450 GPU launch | Positive if benchmarks beat H200; neutral/negative if delayed |
| 2H 2026 | Helios rack-scale platform launch | Positive — multi-GPU hyperscale positioning |
| Ongoing | TSMC tariff exemption decision | Potential positive relief if hyperscaler exemptions enacted |
| Pending | Kevin Warsh Fed chair Senate confirmation | Macro: potential hawkish shift risk |

Sources: web_research/AMD.json run 20260501_133944; swing_catalyst_news signal run 20260501_133944.

## Recent news synthesis (within 14 days)

**Positive:**
- **Intel Q1 blowout read-through** — Intel surged +24.89% on Q1 2026 beat driven by its 18A process node and AI CPU demand, validating x86 AI CPU demand that directly benefits AMD (source: web_research/AMD.json, 20260501_133944).
- **DA Davidson upgrade** — upgraded AMD to Buy, raised PT from $220 to $375, citing structural CPU demand from agentic AI deployment. April 29 (source: web_research/AMD.json, 20260501_133944).
- **Susquehanna upgrade** — reiterated Buy, lifted PT from $300 to $375, citing data center GPU demand ahead of Q1 earnings. April 29 (source: web_research/AMD.json, 20260501_133944).
- **Envision Research upgrade** — upgraded AMD to Buy citing superior positioning in agentic AI infrastructure. April 30 (source: web_research/AMD.json, 20260501_133944).
- **AMD hit ATH ~$353** — +76% April gain, driven by AI capex cycle confirmation (Meta $60B, Amazon, Alphabet all expanding). AMD record Q4 2025 Data Center revenue $5.38B (+34% YoY). (source: web_research/AMD.json, 20260501_133944).
- **Meta $60B infrastructure deal** — multi-year commitment covering AMD GPUs + Venice CPUs + custom silicon co-engineering. Still structural bull catalyst (source: thesis.md; prior runs).

**Negative:**
- **Northland downgrade** — downgraded to Market Perform from Outperform, PT $260, citing competitive landscape headwinds. April 27 (source: web_research/AMD.json, 20260501_133944).
- **Stock above consensus PT** — at $353-357, AMD trades well above average analyst consensus PT ~$293; Evercore ISI bull target is $358. Sell-the-news risk on beat-and-lower Q2 guidance due to 25% tariff headwinds on AI chip exports.
- **25% tariff on advanced AI chips** — ongoing margin headwind on MI325X; cannot fully pass through on hyperscaler contracts (source: swing_catalyst_news, 20260501_133944).

## Analyst consensus (as of May 1, 2026)

CEO Lisa Su sold approximately $16 million in shares in March 2026. Board-level insider distribution: 2:1 sellers vs buyers. Not a near-term directional catalyst but reinforces "don't chase at ATH" posture (source: swing_catalyst_news signal, run 20260501_133944).

## Analyst consensus (as of May 1, 2026)

- **Rating:** Buy consensus — 41% Strong Buy, 38% Buy, 21% Hold, 0% Sell (34-analyst panel, MarketBeat/web_research)
- **Avg price target:** ~$293 (34-analyst consensus); stock at $353-357 is currently above average consensus PT
- **Bull targets:** Melius Research $380, DA Davidson $375, Susquehanna $375, Evercore ISI $358
- **Bear target:** Northland $260 (downgrade April 27); low analyst target $120

Sources: web_research/AMD.json run 20260501_133944; swing_catalyst_news signal run 20260501_133944.

lessons_full.content:
## Format

Each lesson is one dated bullet:

`[DATE] | [TICKER] | [SETUP TYPE] | [OUTCOME: +$X / -$X] | [WHY it worked or failed in 1 sentence]`

The "why" should reference: the thesis at the time, the macro regime, which swing strategy recommended it.

---

## Patterns (auto-generated by Sunday compactor, last 30 days)

| Setup Type | Trades | Wins | Win Rate | Avg P&L | Total P&L |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| EMA pullback Fib dip-buy | 1 | 0 | 0% | -$63.20 | -$63.20 |

## Lessons

2026-04-30 | NVDA | EMA pullback Fib dip-buy | -$63.20 (corrected from -$264.65) | Stop triggered at $205.30 (hourly Fib 61.8%); swing_trend_momentum and swing_breakout were bullish on AI capex thesis, but price breached the stop zone intraday amid stretched macro (RSI 81–87) with no bullish confirmation candle before exit. **Note:** trade was originally sized 67 shares ($14,020) due to a phantom $100K budget bug (fixed commit b2b472d). With the correct $25K budget at 14.2% vol-adj limit, position would have been 16 shares ($3,348). Corrected P&L reflects the strategy's actual signal quality, not the sizing error.
